framing: Royal Navy Warfare Officer transitioning to maritime defence systems role at BAE Systems in Glasgow

jd.title: Senior Systems Engineer — Naval Combat Systems
jd.location: Glasgow, Scotland (Govan Shipyard, SC/DV clearance required)
jd.style_note: Defence-cleared: UK security clearance mandatory, specific naval platform experience, MOD procurement context

requirements.text: Active SC or DV security clearance (or eligibility to obtain)
10+ years of experience in naval combat management systems, platform systems integration, or naval architecture
Knowledge of CMS (Combat Management System) architecture: CMS variants used on Type 26, Type 45, or Queen Elizabeth class
Experience with naval sensor and weapon system integration (radar, sonar, missile systems, electronic warfare)
Understanding of MOD acquisition framework: CADMID lifecycle, DE&S (Defence Equipment & Support) processes
Familiarity with Def Stan 00-56 (Safety Management) and Def Stan 00-55 (Safety Critical Software)
Experience with MBSE tools (Sparx Enterprise Architect, IBM Rhapsody, or Cameo Systems Modeler)
Chartered Engineer (CEng) status with IMarEST or IET

responsibilities: Lead combat system integration for Type 26 Global Combat Ship programme
Develop system requirements and interface specifications for CMS, sensors, and weapons integration
Coordinate with DE&S Naval Authority on technical assurance and safety case development
Support factory acceptance testing (FAT) and harbour acceptance testing (HAT) of combat system equipment
Manage technical interfaces between BAE Systems, Thales UK (sonar), and MBDA (missile systems)

expected_output.summary: Royal Navy officer (Commander, retiring) with 15 years of warfare and combat systems experience. Currently Principal Warfare Officer on a Type 45 destroyer. Previously served as Combat Systems Engineering Officer on Type 23 frigate and as Flag Lieutenant. Extensive operational experience with BAE Systems CMS, Sea Viper missile system, and Artisan 3D radar. SC cleared. Operational deployments to Gulf, Mediterranean, and Indo-Pacific. Seeking transition to defence industry naval combat systems role.

experience.title: Principal Warfare Officer (Commander, OF-4)
bullets: Led warfare department (45 personnel) responsible for operation of all combat systems: CMS, Sea Viper (Aster 15/30), Phalanx CIWS, and EW suite
Directed tactical operations during Indo-Pacific deployment (2023) including air defence exercises with JMSDF and RAN
Managed combat system readiness and maintenance programme achieving 95% system availability
Coordinated with BAE Systems and Thales UK on software updates and system defect resolution during refit periods
Qualified as Anti-Air Warfare Director (AAWD) — Type 45's primary air defence coordination role

experience.title: Combat Systems Engineering Officer (Lieutenant Commander, OF-3)
bullets: Managed combat systems engineering team (18 ratings and 3 officers) for Type 23 frigate's sensor and weapon systems
Oversaw operation and maintenance of Artisan 3D radar, Sonar 2087 (towed array), and Sea Ceptor missile system
Led combat system operational testing during major NATO exercise (Joint Warrior 2021)
Coordinated with HMNB Devonport shore support for combat system deep maintenance and urgent defect repairs

experience.company: Royal Navy — HMS Collingwood (Maritime Warfare School)
experience.title: Warfare Training Officer (Lieutenant, OF-2)
bullets: Delivered Above Water Warfare (AWW) and Electronic Warfare (EW) training to junior warfare officers
Developed tactical simulation scenarios using BAE Systems Tactical Trainer
Completed Principal Warfare Officer course at HMS Collingwood

certifications: NEBOSH General Certificate — Occupational Health and Safety (2021)
education: BEng Marine Engineering — Britannia Royal Naval College / University of Plymouth (2009-2012)
MA Defence Studies — King's College London (distance learning, 2019-2021)

jd_requirements: 10+ years naval combat management platform integration naval architecture
CMS combat management system Type 26 Type 45 Queen Elizabeth
sensor weapon system radar sonar missile electronic warfare integration
MOD acquisition CADMID DE&S Defence Equipment Support
Def Stan 00-56 safety management Def Stan 00-55 safety critical software
MBSE Sparx Enterprise Architect IBM Rhapsody Cameo
deliberate_flaws: No Type 26 experience — served on Type 45 and Type 23 (Type 26 is the programme being recruited for)
No CADMID or DE&S acquisition experience — operational Navy, not MOD procurement
No Def Stan 00-56 or Def Stan 00-55 experience (used systems, didn't develop safety cases)
No MBSE tools experience (Sparx EA, Rhapsody, Cameo) — operational user, not systems engineer
No CEng status — military career didn't include professional engineering registration
No systems requirements development or ICD authoring experience
No factory acceptance or harbour acceptance testing from the contractor side
Military to industry transition — different working culture, commercial vs operational mindset
No experience managing technical interfaces between prime contractors and sub-system suppliers
Warfare Officer background, not engineering branch — deep operational knowledge but limited formal systems engineering methodology
No naval architecture knowledge (JD mentions it as alternative experience path)

provenance.notes: Synthetic persona and JD. JD style modeled from real Greenhouse/Lever postings. Expected output and cloud_skills are AI-generated. 4/8 scorecard checks (jd_coverage, no_fabrication, metrics_preserved, no_fabricated_skills) evaluate AI vs AI reference. 4/8 checks (factual_preservation, word_count, ats_structure, action_verbs) are structurally valid regardless.
